apiKey.description: API key issued to a Sonzai project. Format: `Bearer sk_...`.

bearerClerk.description: Clerk session JWT from an authenticated dashboard user. Format: `Bearer eyJ...`.

info.description: Sonzai Labs Platform API — the B2B customer SDK surface. Use `apiKey` for programmatic/SDK access or `bearerClerk` for dashboard-session calls. Each endpoint's `security` field indicates which schemes are accepted. Internal staff operations live at /api/v1/admin-portal/openapi.json (staff-only). Public unauthenticated storefront operations live at /s/openapi.json.
info.title: Sonzai Platform API

get.description: Returns all configuration entries for the authenticated tenant as a `{key: json}` map. Free-form values.

delete.description: Removes the config entry for the authenticated tenant. 204 No Content on success.

get.description: Returns the raw JSON value stored under the given key for the authenticated tenant. 404 when the key is not set.

put.description: Stores a raw JSON value under the given key for the authenticated tenant. Body must be valid JSON (≤ 1 MB).

get.description: Returns agents for the caller's tenant, optionally filtered by project. Supports cursor-based pagination (page_size+cursor) and legacy offset-based pagination (limit+offset).

post.description: Creates a new agent (or updates if agent_id/name already exists). Handles profile creation in ScyllaDB and index upsert in CockroachDB. Optional lore generation, avatar generation, and initial goals are fired in background goroutines.
Combines generate-character + create in one idempotent call. If the agent already exists with a valid profile, returns the existing agent without calling the LLM. Safe to call on every app startup.
post.summary: Generate a character and create the agent

post.description: Uses an LLM to produce a structured character profile (bio, Big5, traits, etc.) from a short description. If the agent already exists and `regenerate` is false, returns the existing profile.
post.summary: Generate a character profile from a description

post.description: Returns personality profiles and evolution counts for up to 50 agents in a single request.

delete.description: Permanently removes an agent and ALL associated data from ScyllaDB (profiles, memory, habits, goals, etc.), CockroachDB (index, instances, custom states), and GCS (generated files).

parameters.description: Agent UUID or URL-encoded agent name
parameters.name: agentId

schema.description: Agent UUID or URL-encoded agent name

delete.summary: Delete an agent and all data

get.description: Returns the agent's index row merged with the ScyllaDB profile (bio, gender, avatar, personality, traits). The ScyllaDB profile fetch is best-effort: a missing or unreachable profile does not fail the request.

parameters.description: Agent UUID or URL-encoded agent name (resolved against the caller's tenant)
parameters.name: agentId

schema.description: Agent UUID or URL-encoded agent name (resolved against the caller's tenant)

get.summary: Get a single agent by ID

post.description: Generates a new avatar for the agent using personality data. Only one generation per agent at a time (concurrent requests return 409 Conflict).

schema.description: Agent UUID or URL-encoded agent name

post.description: Proxies to the AI service to generate a bio for the agent using personality data. Supports both orchestrator (pre-built enriched context) and workbench (minimal context) usage.

post.description: Makes the storefront live at /s/{slug}. Validates that slug is set and at least one agent is placed.

get.description: Returns tickets created by the authenticated user within their active tenant. Filter by `status` or `type`; paginate with `limit`/`offset`.

parameters.description: Filter by status (open, in_progress, resolved, closed)
parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status (open, in_progress, resolved, closed)

parameters.description: Filter by type (support, bug, feature_request, billing, ...)
parameters.name: type

schema.description: Filter by type (support, bug, feature_request, billing, ...)

post.description: Creates a support ticket in the caller's tenant. `type` defaults to `support` and `priority` to `medium` when omitted.

get.description: Returns the ticket and its comment thread. 404 when the ticket belongs to a different tenant.
get.summary: Get a support ticket with comments

post.description: Closes the ticket if the caller is its original creator. Returns 403 when the caller is in the tenant but did not create the ticket.
Appends a user comment to the ticket thread. User comments are always external (`is_internal=false`); only staff can create internal comments via the admin portal.
post.summary: Add a comment to a support ticket

get.description: Returns all tenants the authenticated dashboard user has access to. Used by the org-switcher.
Returns tenant metadata (name, slug, created_at) for the given tenant ID.
Returns active nodes stored at scope_id="" for the given tenant. Used by the admin dashboard's cascade browser.

post.description: Writes a node at scope_id="" so every project under the tenant can read it via cascade / union scope modes. Idempotency is the caller's responsibility; a dashboard should look up by label before calling this.

get.description: Returns all user personas owned by the caller's tenant. Auto-seeds a default set on first access.

post.description: Creates a new persona in the caller's tenant. Name is required.

delete.description: Deletes a persona after verifying tenant ownership.

get.description: Returns a single persona by ID. 404 when the persona belongs to a different tenant.

put.description: Updates name, description, and/or style. `is_default` is preserved from the existing row.

get.description: Returns the list of available Gemini voices for text-to-speech. Optionally filter by gender.
Returns all registered webhooks for the project resolved from the API key's tenant context.

delete.description: Removes the webhook for the project resolved from the API key's tenant context.

put.description: Creates or updates a webhook for the project resolved from the API key's tenant context.

get.description: Returns recent delivery attempts for the project resolved from the API key's tenant context.

post.description: Generates a new HMAC signing secret for the project resolved from the API key's tenant context.

parameters.name: eventType

schema.description: Webhook event type whose secret to rotate

post.description: Runs the same background CE workers (diary, consolidation, constellation) the production scheduler would run, for a chosen number of simulated days. Used by the workbench time-machine UI and long-horizon benchmarks. Pass `async: true` to run in the background and poll `/workbench/advance-time/jobs/{jobId}` — useful when the full run exceeds your proxy's read timeout.
post.summary: Advance simulated time in the workbench

get.description: Returns the current state of an async advance-time job started via POST /workbench/advance-time with async=true. Status is one of 'running', 'succeeded', or 'failed'. Job state lives in Redis with a 30-minute TTL — poll within that window.

post.description: Streams chat with the same pipeline as production /chat but with inspector-level trace events interleaved on the stream. TODO: typed chunk schema + SDK polish.

200.description: SSE stream of workbench chat chunks (same framing as /chat).

post.description: Generates a short bio for the proposed agent personality. Used by the character-builder workflow.
One-shot character generator. Drives the dashboard character-builder's "surprise me" flow.
post.summary: Generate a full character (personality + bio + seed memories)

post.description: Generates a list of seed memories (origin stories, key lore) for the proposed agent personality.
Warms context-engine caches for the given agent+user before a chat run. Returns the runId to use in subsequent workbench calls.
post.summary: Prepare the workbench for a run

post.description: Deletes the ephemeral workbench agent's profile, memories, and sessions so a fresh run starts from zero state.
Runs the same session-end pipeline production uses after ~4 min inactivity. Returns the resulting consolidation and diary entries.
Generates the next user turn from a synthetic persona. Used by the workbench simulator and by SDK integration tests.
Returns the current workbench state for inspection (loaded facts, sessions, pending jobs). Used by the dashboard inspector pane.
